output.explanation: All other values follow the pattern: two-letter airline code, then a numeric identifier with 1+ digits, then three-letter IATA codes separated by hyphens (e.g., 'AA-xxxx-AAA-BBB', 'UA-xxx-AAA-BBB', 'CO-xxxx-AAA-BBB'). This value uses 'ORF' instead of a three-letter destination consistent with the other airport codes (most values end with 'MCO','ORD','DTW','PHL','CLT','SFO','LAX','IAH','JFK','MIA','DFW','PHX','DEN','EWR','CLE','BOS','ATL','MSP','SLC','SNA','CVG','CVG','FLL','GUA','MRY','PVG','EDI','YYC','HOU','DAL','EWR','ORF never appears elsewhere).

All other values use 1+ digits for the flight number segment after the airline code and before the first hyphen (e.g., 'AA-3-JFK-LAX', 'AA-59-JFK-SFO'). This value has a nonstandard single-digit airline/flight segment with an extra hyphen count compared to the dominant pattern seen across the list (it still has 4 hyphen-separated segments, but 'AA-3-JFK-LAX' is an outlier); repair to match the prevailing multi-digit numeric format by padding with a leading zero is the syntactic fix most consistent with the rest of the values.
